rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving references to visible identifiers in the current component scope
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model
Move implementation-level bindings to implementation scope and avoid placing guarantees in implementation annexes
Ensure AGREE scalar types match AADL expectations (int, real, bool)
generic_rules: Validate that all identifiers in AGREE contracts are declared or visible in the current scope
Use only valid AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions
Avoid introducing new data types or packages in AGREE repairs
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and their expressions
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared variables
Guarantees should be placed in component type scopes unless referencing implementation-level details
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid references to AADL-specific type names
anti_patterns: Do not add new package references or data types not present in the AADL model
Do not place assignment statements in component type annexes
Do not invent new identifiers or types in AGREE repairs
Do not assume that unresolved references can be fixed by adding declarations
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator errors about mismatched input or rule ID expectations
Model fusion or component type/implementation scope boundaries
